summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a brief regular meeting primarily to address a proposed M3 Limited High Impact Industrial district text amendment (Case 2026). Due to the complexity of the amendment and ongoing coordination with the County Prosecutor's Office, staff requested a continuance to the June 2nd meeting, which was unanimously approved. The meeting adjourned after only four minutes.
decisions: Motion to continue Zoning Case 2026 (M3 Limited High Impact Industrial text amendment) to June 2, 2026 (unanimous)
action_items: John (staff) to finalize and clean up the M3 text amendment draft including graphs, tables, and cross-references for June 2nd hearing
Staff to continue coordinating with Township legal department and County Prosecutor's Office on the M3 amendment language
Staff to prepare clarified materials for public review ahead of the June 2, 2026 meeting
notable_topics: M3 Limited High Impact Industrial district text amendment (Case 2026)
New zoning district with performance standards, buffer requirements, parking access standards, and cross-references to multiple articles
Alignment of M3 standards with existing M1 and M2 provisions
Board of Trustees Resolution R-012-2026 adopted March 24, 2026 referring the amendment to Zoning Commission
notes: Mr. Frisbee's name was called during roll call but no response was recorded, suggesting he was absent. The meeting lasted only about 4 minutes (6:30 to 6:34 PM). The M3 amendment affects articles 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 13, 16 with exhibits A through M. A potential applicant may be interested in a July meeting slot.
title: ZC 5-5-2026
